Among these various political movements emerged the National Socialist German Workers' Party (NSDAP), founded in 1920 by Adolf Hitler - an Austrian-born revolutionary seeking national revival through social Darwinism and Aryan supremacy narratives to promote racial purity above all. The party quickly gained momentum as it capitalized on anti-Semitic sentiments, promising to restore Germany's former glory by addressing the perceived failures of Weimar Republic policies in tackling unemployment, hyperinflation, and social issues amongst other grievances.

In 1932, Hitler was appointed as Chancellor under a Nazi-led coalition government after securing electoral promises to dismantle the Treaty of Versailles by remilitarizing Germany; he then proceeded systematically to consolidate power through Gleichschaltung - merging all political entities with National Socialism and eradicating opposition parties. By 1933, when Hitler took full control as Führer (Leader), the Nazi propaganda machine was in overdrive: glorifying war-hero Aryan ancestors while demonizing Jews - portraying them as enemies of Germany's economic recovery and cultural renaissance.

The Gleichschaltung process allowed Himmler, Reinhard Heydrich, and other key figures like Heinrich Himmler (Reichsführer SS), Rudolf Hess, Ernst Kaltenbrunner to implement brutal anti-Semitic policies without fear of consequences or opposition - an era when the term "final solution" first emerged in official Nazi parlance. These men became instrumental architects behind one of history's most horrific crimes against humanity: The systematic extermination, torture and exploitation of millions based on racial categorization.

Eichmann was appointed as head of logistics for the Final Solution; his efficient bureaucracy facilitated mass deportations to concentration camps across Europe where Jews were subjected to unspeakable horrors with impunity - an example being Auschwitz-Birkenau, run under Heinrich Himmler's command. Hitler and other high ranking Nazi officials maintained a perverse sense of pride in these atrocities as they continued their regime's genocidal policies while presenting themselves as saviors restoring Germany to greatness through Aryan supremess.
